title: 2020 AMC 12A Problems/Problem 13
problem: There are integers $a, b,$ and $c,$ each greater than $1,$ such that \[\sqrt[a]{N\sqrt[b]{N\sqrt[c]{N}}} = \sqrt[36]{N^{25}}\] for all $N \neq 1$ . What is $b$ ? $\textbf{(A) } 2 \qquad \textbf{(B) } 3 \qquad \textbf{(C) } 4 \qquad \textbf{(D) } 5 \qquad \textbf{(E) } 6$
solution: $\sqrt[a]{N\sqrt[b]{N\sqrt[c]{N}}}$ can be simplified to $N^{\frac{1}{a}+\frac{1}{ab}+\frac{1}{abc}}.$ The equation is then $N^{\frac{1}{a}+\frac{1}{ab}+\frac{1}{abc}}=N^{\frac{25}{36}}$ which implies that $\frac{1}{a}+\frac{1}{ab}+\frac{1}{abc}=\frac{25}{36}.$ $a$ has to be $2$ since $\frac{25}{36}>\frac{7}{12}$ . $\frac{7}{12}$ is the result when $a, b,$ and $c$ are $3, 2,$ and $2$ $b$ being $3$ will make the fraction $\frac{2}{3}$ which is close to $\frac{25}{36}$ . Finally, with $c$ being $6$ , the fraction becomes $\frac{25}{36}$ . In this case $a, b,$ and $c$ work, which means that $b$ must equal $\boxed{\textbf{(B) } 3.}$ ~lopkiloinm
